note.summary: PathUtils.is_passable must return false for out-of-bounds tiles; treating them as passable lets BFS/pathfinding escape the room grid and hang room generation and targeting.

RunEngine, CombatEngine, RoomGenerator, and ProgressionStore own all gameplay rules in pure RefCounted scripts over dictionaries so the same codepath powers both the playable scenes and headless regression tests.
Traversal stays on the room board: route choices come from clicking highlighted door tiles in room mode, while the minimap remains non-interactive and only provides orientation/context.
UI validation relies on screenshot probes for the menu, room start, combat, and post-card states so layout changes can be judged visually even in headless-first sessions.
UI copy should stay terse and action-oriented; prefer visual communication, concise labels, and lightweight overlays over explanatory panel text or multi-sentence instructions.

RunScene builds the camp-menu and pile-inspector overlays in code; resume vs. new-run flow is chosen from the root meta key 'labyrinth_resume_saved_run' and backed by ProgressionStore's separate saved-run slot.

UI regressions should be checked with screenshot probes for combat, targeting, after-play, the camp menu, and pile inspectors, because key overlays are built in code and scene-instantiation tests alone will miss visual breakage.
The project theme must reference fonts/PressStart2P-Regular.tres, not the raw .ttf; Godot's custom project theme loading fails to resolve the .ttf as a FontFile in headless/tests.

RunScene sizes MiniMapOverlay in _layout_mini_map_overlay() from the current viewport instead of fixed offsets because a larger static corner map was overflowing smaller windows and obscuring the board.

Combat hands are only dead when both the printed card previews and the drag fallback previews are invalid; Pass logic and hand dimming need to account for the alternate 2-attack and 2-move plays, not just the printed card text.

Pixel-font cards need a tall card ratio and clipped contents; near-square hand/modal cards cause the title, art, and footer to spill, so the shared CardWidget is tuned around concise action summaries and roughly 1.4x height-to-width sizing.

Block behavior is codified in regression tests: player block must absorb damage across the whole enemy phase, and enemy block must persist into the next player turn until it is spent by incoming damage or cleared when that enemy acts again.

Combat hands are only dead when both the printed card previews and the drag fallback previews are invalid; Pass logic and hand dimming need to account for the alternate 2-attack and 2-move plays, not just the printed card text.

UI regressions need screenshot probes for combat, pile inspectors, the drag overlay, fallback targeting, and after-play states because those overlays are built in code and scene-instantiation tests miss layout and input breakage.

Multi-action cards treat targeted steps as skippable by default unless an action is marked required; the preview auto-skips dead target steps and the combat choice bar exposes Skip for optional follow-ups that still have valid targets.

Enemy block is preview-time: assigning a revealed intent preloads any block onto the enemy for the current player turn, then enemy phase clears that preview block before actions resolve so it does not carry into the next turn.

Regression tests now lock the updated combat rules: revealed enemy block must mitigate the current player turn immediately, and multi-action cards must stay playable when optional targeted steps can be skipped or auto-skipped.

Targetless cards must commit on the initial click path itself; previewing their resolved state without immediately awaiting _play_player_card leaves a ghost preview that cancels on the next selection instead of spending the card.
Hand-card damage text should come from CombatEngine final-damage and modifier helpers, using a preview-state copy so Ember Lens stays aligned with live combat on multi-action cards.
UI probes must clear saved runs and load a fresh deterministic run state before capturing; otherwise screenshots can silently reuse stale progress and mask changes like enemy-density or HUD updates.
Drag-overlay probes must place the drag proxy explicitly
note.details: The UI probe does not move the real mouse, so drag-overlay screenshots can miss the dragged card unless the probe calls _update_drag_proxy_position before capture. Keep doing that when visually verifying drag-and-drop regressions.

note.summary: Pile widgets need collapsed legacy children and short empty captions
note.details: The dynamic draw/discard/burn widgets still sit on top of the original scene controls, so _build_pile_widget should zero out those legacy children. Empty-pile captions also need to stay short (DECK/DISC/BURN); letting discard render DISCARD can overflow and look like a stretched pile even when the frame is fine.

note.summary: Stable card drag needs a top-level proxy and hidden source card
note.details: The dragged card proxy should be top_level and the source hand widget should be hidden instead of rebuilding the whole hand at drag start. Rebuilding under the cursor or leaving the source visible causes jittery drag behavior and visual fighting between the proxy and the hand layout.

note.summary: Hover-lifted cards need unclipped transparent hand scroll
note.details: The hand ScrollContainer should use an empty panel style and clip_contents=false. Otherwise hovered cards get their tops cut off and the hand row can show a darker panel strip that reads like a mismatched background behind the cards.

note.summary: Stable card drag needs a top-level proxy and hidden source card
note.details: The dragged card proxy should be top_level and the source hand widget should be hidden instead of rebuilding the whole hand at drag start. Rebuilding under the cursor or leaving the source visible causes jittery drag behavior and visual fighting between the proxy and the hand layout.

note.summary: Top-level card proxies must not reuse position-based hover lift
note.details: CardWidget's hover/selection pose adjusts position.y, which is fine inside the hand layout but breaks top_level drag proxies by snapping them toward y=0. For drag proxies, keep absolute position untouched and only apply safe visual properties like scale.

note.summary: Elemental combat stores burn, freeze, shock, poison, and stoneskin directly on unit dictionaries so cards, room-tuned enemy intents, board badges, and tests all share one status model.

Enemy threat previews follow the hovered enemy's current intent, keep move_toward/move_away directional, and exclude move tiles from attack tiles so blue movement and red threat overlays stay distinct.

Player freeze and shock badges need to merge player_turn_restrictions with the stored status counters, because prepare_next_player_turn consumes those counters before the board renders the locked turn.
Enemy attack steps now enqueue only when the player state actually changes, and shallow elemental rooms tone down enemy status payloads so freeze/shock pressure ramps up later in the run.
Ice and lightning enemy CC now comes only from rarer intents (weight 2 or less) at depth 3+, and those freeze/shock attacks cap their range at 4 so elemental control pressure ramps more gently.

ActionIconLibrary is the shared source of truth for reusable combat keyword icon tokens, paths, and tooltips; card summaries and combat board intents/status badges should consume its token rows instead of hand-building keyword strings.
Keep CardWidget compact cutoff below normal hand width
note.details: Combat hand cards normally max at 160px wide, so treating 160px as compact makes the icon summary rows render small. The rarity footer is hidden to reserve vertical space for larger icon rows.

note.summary: Use UiTooltipPanel for custom tooltip overlays
note.details: Cards and custom-drawn combat board regions should route keyword and modifier tooltip text through UiTooltipPanel so tooltip styling stays consistent and avoids the default translucent black engine tooltip.

note.summary: Depth-sort board bodies and fade foreground blockers
note.details: CombatBoardView draws floor/overlays first, then interleaves terrain props and unit bodies in tile draw order. Unit HUDs stay in a final pass for readability. Tall blockers (wall/pillar) tint translucent only when their prop rect overlaps a unit on a farther-back tile, using the same tile comparator as rendering.

note.summary: The reaver idle sheet is exported column-major (top-to-bottom, then left-to-right), so CombatBoardView must reorder frames instead of using the default row-major atlas order.

reason: Atlas analysis and in-engine playback verification showed the smoothest 8-frame idle loop is standard row-major order.

note.summary: The reaver idle sheet should be read in standard row-major order (left-to-right, then top-to-bottom); reordering it column-major makes the idle loop visibly less smooth.
CombatBoardView overrides the final frame of player_reaver_idle with the penultimate frame because the exported last pose makes the sword pop abruptly in motion.
CombatBoardView plays player_reaver_idle as a ping-pong loop (0..7..1) so the idle bob reverses cleanly instead of snapping from the last pose back to the first.

reason: Ping-pong looping did not improve the perceived hitch enough to keep, so CombatBoardView was reverted to the straightforward forward row-major idle loop.

note.summary: Enemy intent popups reserve a title line for the intent name, including name-only intents like Wait, so layout code and tests should size popups from total intent lines instead of raw action-row count.

Per-enemy sprite sizing is driven by enemy_def art_scale and applied after fitting the texture, scaling around the fitted rect center so smaller variants stay tile-centered and keep their HUD alignment.
Enemy art_scale is applied after fitting, anchored at the fitted rect bottom-center so smaller sprites stay centered horizontally, keep their feet planted, and keep HUD placement stable.
Enemy intent popups now auto-expand to fit their title and token rows, preserving a fixed minimum width while preventing long names like Skitter Strike from clipping.
CombatBoardView auto-loads same-stem _idle sheets into 4x2 frame loops and only uses those frames when a unit is not currently being moved or focused by combat presentation.
Move and attack targeting now rely on tile highlights and path lines only; CombatBoardView no longer draws extra preview glyphs like circles or plus signs over target tiles.
Auto room dialogue is keyed by room coord plus turns_spent so _refresh_ui() can safely rerun without reopening the overlay, while revisiting the same room later still retriggers the conversation.

RunEngine traversal is now a sealed ring graph: non-center rooms keep east/west same-depth links, only one quarter of each ring links deeper, and move filtering permanently blocks any room once you leave it or any exit toward lower depth.

RunEngine traversal now uses square Chebyshev rings with 8*d rooms per depth, cardinal-only perimeter links, 1/4 of each ring linking outward, inward moves locked after advancing, and empty treasure rooms falling back to room mode.
Combat board unit art now honors per-definition art_offset_x/y after fit/scale; adjust sprite centering in enemy/npc data instead of changing global anchoring.
note.details: Introduced for the Ash Acolyte PNG alignment and covered by _test_enemy_art_offset_shifts_sprite_vertically in tests/run_tests.gd.

note.summary: CombatBoardView idle sheets are now data-driven per unit: definitions can override idle_sheet_columns, idle_sheet_rows, and idle_sheet_order while same-stem _idle loading remains automatic.
note.details: Default behavior stays 4x2 row-major, but Ash Acolyte uses 2x4 column_major and is covered by _test_acolyte_idle_sheet_honors_column_major_layout in tests/run_tests.gd.

note.summary: CombatBoardView idle animation is fully data-driven per unit: definitions can override idle_sheet_columns, idle_sheet_rows, idle_sheet_order, and idle_frame_seconds while same-stem _idle loading remains automatic.
note.details: Default behavior stays 4x2 row-major at 0.10s per frame, but Ash Acolyte uses 2x4 column_major at 0.20s per frame; covered by _test_acolyte_idle_sheet_honors_column_major_layout and _test_acolyte_idle_speed_override in tests/run_tests.gd.

note.summary: Door tiles are blocking terrain for PathUtils, so movement, spawns, threat ranges, and pathfinding all treat room exits like walls.
note.details: This prevents enemies from spawning or walking onto exit doors and is covered by _test_room_generation_blocks_door_tiles in tests/run_tests.gd.

note.summary: Enemy HUD collision avoidance moves the full enemy health-plus-intent stack together and draws a tether line when displaced, so overlap fixes still preserve which unit the intent belongs to.
note.details: CombatBoardView scores small sideways/upward nudges against reserved HUD rects, then falls back to a dynamic escape offset based on the stack bounds for dense overlaps; covered by _test_enemy_hud_layout_stays_centered_when_clear and _test_enemy_hud_layout_offsets_away_from_reserved_ui in tests/run_tests.gd.

note.summary: NPC combat art basenames can inherit idle sheets by stem, so static placeholder NPCs need a unique art filename with no matching *_idle texture.
note.details: CombatBoardView auto-loads %s_idle from the art_path basename for both enemies and NPCs. Pointing the Emaciated Man at acolyte.svg pulled in acolyte_idle.png and caused flickering; emaciated_man.svg is intentionally a unique basename with no idle companion.

note.summary: Combat board door visibility is presentation-driven, not layout-driven.
note.details: Room layouts still stamp door blockers into the grid for topology/pathing, but the board renderer shows a wall unless the tile is an active exit or a sealed previously visited door from run_scene locked_door_tiles. Moving units also derive draw order from unit_world_positions so enemy move tweens do not pop back at the end.

note.summary: Multi-action card resolution keeps a target slot per targeted step, using INVALID_TARGET placeholders for skipped steps.
note.details: RunScene now pads auto-skipped and manually skipped targeted actions with INVALID_TARGET_TILE so animation playback stays aligned with the original action list, which enables one-click move-then-attack enemy shortcuts without misassigning later targets.

note.summary: Combat board door visibility and move layering are presentation-driven via explicit door and draw-tile maps.
note.details: run_scene now injects active_door_tiles for connected exits that should still look like doors during combat, locked_door_tiles for sealed traversal doors, and unit_draw_tiles for animated movers so CombatBoardView can keep smooth world-position interpolation without nearest-tile snapping.

note.summary: Health bar HP text should use a 1px bottom inset instead of the old fixed +10 baseline, or the outlined Press Start 2P digits clip against the top border.
note.details: The HP readout is drawn inside a 12px bar with a 1px four-direction outline; bottom-anchoring the baseline at rect.size.y - 1 keeps the numerals visually centered and avoids the top-edge clipping that shows up with this pixel font.

note.summary: Enemy health bars intentionally use a larger 84x14 rect while player bars stay 78x12, because the outlined HP digits still feel cramped in a 12px enemy bar.
note.details: CombatBoardView bottom-anchors both bars to the same HUD clearance, so enlarging the enemy bar grows it upward into free space without pushing it down into sprite art or breaking intent stacking.

note.summary: Trap movement paths now minimize trap crossings before distance, and trap-applied freeze/shock/stun on players are deferred through pending_player_trap_restriction so they hit the next possible action after the current card resolves.
note.details: Voluntary player move previews and move resolution share path_for_player_action, so the previewed route matches the actual step-by-step trap triggers. Enemies treat trap tiles as blocked for intent previews and voluntary movement, but forced movement can still push or pull units through traps and trigger them immediately.

note.summary: move_to_room must refresh its local rooms snapshot after _reveal_neighbors before non-combat room branches mutate room state, or treasure/campfire/NPC transitions can overwrite newly revealed exits and strand the run in a false dead end.
Acolytes now drift forward on every intent and wardens step forward even on Bulwark turns so low-mobility enemies keep closing space without matching crawler speed.
note.details: The balance target is acolyte average toward-move 1.0 and warden average toward-move 1.5, both still below crawler's 1.75. tests/run_tests.gd locks this pacing so these archetypes stop corner camping without becoming rushdown enemies.

note.summary: Low-mobility enemy movement is now varied by intent: acolyte steps 2/1/3 for a two-tile average, while warden steps 1/0/2 for a one-tile average.
note.details: tests/run_tests.gd now checks per-intent movement variety plus simple per-intent averages, while still guarding that wardens remain slower than crawlers in weighted intent frequency.

note.summary: Crawler intent pacing now leads the roster at 3/2/4 movement with slightly lighter 3/5 melee damage, while acolyte and warden stay at 2 and 1 average movement respectively.
note.details: tests/run_tests.gd checks crawler/acolyte/warden per-intent movement averages and weighted ordering so the crawler stays the fastest pressure enemy without matching the larger enemies' damage ceiling.

note.summary: Ash floor rendering now uses seven extracted PNG variants chosen deterministically per room coordinate, with left/up neighbor de-clumping to avoid obvious repeats.
note.details: CombatBoardView loads res://assets/placeholders/tiles/base_floor_tile_01..07.png, trims each texture to its used rect, precomputes a _floor_variant_by_tile lookup in set_combat_state, and falls back to the first variant when indexing misses.

note.summary: Ash floor variants are extracted as isolated tile-only PNGs and loaded untrimmed so every stone keeps the same isometric canvas and avoids neighbor-fragment warping.
note.details: The first extraction pass kept surrounding source context inside the diamond, which could make a variant look peeled or skewed in-game. The corrected assets flood-fill the selected tile component from base_floor_tiles.png, keep neighboring tiles transparent, and CombatBoardView now loads the variant textures without trim_texture_to_used_rect.

note.summary: Room floors now generate as ash with deterministic moss accents, while _base_grid still consumes one legacy floor-theme roll so template and encounter RNG stay aligned with prior room seeds.
note.details: RoomGenerator applies moss after template stamping using a room-seeded coordinate hash plus light neighbor de-clumping, keeps entry tiles ash for readability, and never emits ember floor tiles from generation.

note.summary: Card balance work now uses spec/card_balance_heuristic.md and tools/card_heuristic.py as the default curve-check workflow.
note.details: AGENTS.md tells future sessions to consult the heuristic when creating or modifying cards and to update both the doc and tool whenever combat pacing, status behavior, room spacing, enemy assumptions, fatigue, or action keywords change.

note.summary: The April 2026 card rebalance preserves standout freeze, shock, poison, and mobility identities by taxing the hottest cards with health costs instead of stripping effects.
note.details: Weak mobility and melee cards were buffed with cleaner identity-aligned changes: Shadow Step became a pure blink, Venom Claw and Tectonic Maul stayed adjacent melee and gained separate setup/payload, and underperforming air manipulation cards picked up real damage or flow so the heuristic bands close without turning melee attacks into pseudo-ranged cards.

note.summary: Local card analytics now emit append-only JSONL events through scripts/analytics_store.gd.
note.details: RunScene owns the instrumentation because it can observe reward picks and before/after combat state. It assigns run/combat ids plus per-combat card instance ids, then logs run/combat/reward events together with card_drawn, card_became_playable, card_played, and enemy_status_tick for offline balancing analysis. spec/analytics.md documents schema version 1; delayed status ticks are currently combat-level rather than card-source attributed.

note.summary: Board props now use trimmed PNG art fitted bottom-center in tall tile frames, and visible doors reuse the wall-height prop frame.
note.details: This supports the new 255x255 wall, door, and pillar pixel-art assets without stretching them or rendering doors as low floor markers.

note.summary: Wall and door props now choose row-vs-column orientations from neighboring wall continuity; the perpendicular variant is a mirrored companion of the same processed base art.
note.details: Top/bottom runs use the base wall or door texture, side runs use a horizontal flip for exact style matching, and visible doors still render in the same tall prop frame as walls.

note.summary: Boundary wall corners should follow the later-drawn edge orientation, or mirrored wall segments visibly overhang at room corners.
note.details: CombatBoardView sorts board tiles by x+y and then x, so the corner fallback in _prop_orientation_variant needs to choose the edge that draws later rather than whichever boundary test runs first. The integrated door art also keeps the same trimmed footprint as wall.png so door tiles inherit the same tall prop frame without spilling past the tile.

note.summary: Boundary wall and door props render with a narrower draw width than their raw fitted texture so each blocker stays inside a single wall tile span.
note.details: Wall segments still use the tall prop frame and bottom alignment, but _prop_draw_rect now horizontally compresses boundary blockers after fitting: walls use a slightly reduced width and doors use an even narrower inset width so the opening reads as part of one wall segment instead of spilling across neighbors.

note.summary: Prop-art regression tests should compare trimmed pillar-vs-wall silhouette rather than assuming the pillar stays perfectly square after texture trimming.
note.details: CombatBoardView trims PNG props to their used rect before fitting. The pillar asset loads with a noticeably squarer silhouette than the wall art, but not a perfect 1:1 ratio, so regression coverage should assert that pillars stay materially broader and squarer than wall segments instead of hard-coding a square threshold.

note.summary: Room generation now reserves wall tiles for the perimeter and converts interior template walls into pillars.
note.details: The template library still defines obstacle layouts, but any interior TILE_WALL entry is remapped to TILE_PILLAR during generation so combat rooms only use walls on the outer ring while pillars remain the only interior blocking terrain.

note.summary: Perimeter walls and doors now render as explicit boundary segments, and corner tiles split into two half-segments that meet at the tile center.
note.details: CombatBoardView no longer picks a single wall orientation per boundary tile. Straight perimeter tiles draw one full row or column segment, while corner tiles draw two cropped half-segments from the existing wall art so the outer runs meet cleanly in the middle of the corner tile without the old seam gap.

note.summary: Room moss now lives in decorative metadata instead of terrain tile IDs.
note.details: RoomGenerator returns moss.floor/wall/pillar tile lists, CombatEngine/RunEngine carry that metadata through room and combat state, and CombatBoardView renders seeded moss overlay textures on ash floors plus wall/pillar props. This replaces the old moss-terrain approach so passability and floor variants stay purely stone.

note.summary: Outer boundary wall and door art can stay fully implemented while the default board rendering collapses those edge tiles back to ash.
note.details: CombatBoardView keeps the boundary-segment wall/door helpers and tests, but OUTER_WALL_RENDERING_ENABLED is currently false, so _display_tile_id maps outer wall/door tiles to ash for presentation. This hides the perimeter blockers visually without changing the underlying grid passability or deleting the wall rendering code.

note.summary: With outer wall rendering disabled, CombatBoardView now hides boundary wall tiles by filtering them out of draw and hit-test order while keeping visible doors rendered and clickable.
note.details: _display_tile_id still resolves hidden boundary doors back to wall semantics, but _tiles_in_draw_order now applies _should_render_tile so only true boundary walls disappear. Active exits and locked presentation doors stay as "door" tiles, which removes the old fake ash perimeter without breaking traversal.

note.summary: Visible doors now render as standalone props with a dedicated frame instead of reusing wall-segment rendering.
note.details: CombatBoardView draws visible "door" tiles from the standalone door.png texture on their own centered frame, leaves the wall boundary-segment path for actual walls only, and reuses the same door sprite for every edge instead of mirroring wall-insert art. This replaced the older wall-height door treatment once the user swapped in a self-contained doorway asset.

note.summary: Door presentation now uses a cleaned transparent source plus skewed row/col variants derived from door.png for diagonal boundary exits.
note.details: The user-supplied door.png had an opaque fake checkerboard background, so the asset was cleaned to real alpha and used as the source/fallback door art. CombatBoardView now loads dedicated door_row.png and door_col.png transformed from that source and renders visible boundary doors through boundary_prop_segments again, so left/right edge doors follow the board perspective instead of reusing the same upright module everywhere.

note.summary: Door presentation uses the supplied isometric door.png directly on ash floor tiles, flipped horizontally for the opposite diagonal.
note.details: After the user replaced door.png with a transparent tile-oriented asset, CombatBoardView stopped loading generated door_row.png/door_col.png skew variants. Visible doors now draw as a single overlay prop on the regular ash floor tile; row-edge doors use door.png as-is for the bottom-left/top-right diagonal, and col-edge doors use AssetLoader.flip_texture_h for the bottom-right/top-left diagonal.

note.summary: Door size and placement are hand-tuned through explicit frame scale constants.
note.details: CombatBoardView uses DOOR_FRAME_WIDTH_SCALE, DOOR_FRAME_HEIGHT_SCALE, and DOOR_BASELINE_OFFSET_SCALE near the top of the file to fit door.png on top of regular ash floor tiles. Increasing width/height scales the rendered door; changing baseline moves the planted floor line without touching the source asset or flip logic.

note.summary: Door exits show only floating element icons, with rectangular exit label boxes disabled.
note.details: CombatBoardView no longer calls _draw_exit_marker_for_tile from _draw_scene_objects, so visible door exits keep their icon drawn by _draw_tile_props but no longer render the direction/element label box. The marker function remains in the file if labels need to be restored later.

note.summary: AOE card actions use explicit pattern offsets: range 0 resolves from the player without a target, while range > 0 targets a visible center tile; rotate=true patterns auto-select the rotation that hits the most relevant units.
note.details: The card/UI schema stores pattern as JSON offset pairs and optional rotate. CombatEngine validates ranged AOE centers by range/LOS, applies close AOE from the player tile, and exposes aoe_tiles_for_player_action so RunScene previews and animations use the same best-rotation tile set as damage resolution.

note.summary: Permanent card upgrades keep base card ids and resolve through progression.
note.details: Card upgrade purchases are stored in progression.card_upgrades as base_card_id -> upgrade_id. Decks, rewards, analytics card ids, and saved runs keep base card ids; callers resolve display and combat behavior through GameData.card_def_with_upgrades/card_def_for_progression so collectible upgrades do not add those cards to the starting deck.

note.summary: Door props slide toward each perimeter tile's mirrored back edge.
note.details: CombatBoardView offsets visible door frames by half a tile step toward the outside/back edge for all four perimeter sides: top-right up/right, bottom-right down/right, bottom-left down/left, and top-left up/left. Keep DOOR_BACK_EDGE_OFFSET_SCALE side-aware when retuning door placement so mirrored door orientations preserve the same apparent back-edge alignment.

note.summary: Door props use mirrored back-edge offsets with a smaller top-edge scale.
note.details: CombatBoardView offsets visible door frames toward the outside/back edge, but the top pair are tuned slightly forward with DOOR_TOP_BACK_EDGE_OFFSET_SCALE while the right and bottom perimeter doors keep DOOR_BACK_EDGE_OFFSET_SCALE. Keep top-right/top-left mirrored to each other and bottom-right/bottom-left mirrored to each other when retuning door placement.

note.summary: Room door and minimap icons are centralized in RoomIconLibrary.
note.details: RunScene sends RoomIconLibrary icon ids to CombatBoardView for room-exit door icons, and LabyrinthMapView uses the same helper for minimap room icons. Keep non-combat destination icons and minimap icons in this helper so door selection and the map stay visually consistent; cleared minimap rooms should be shown by muted grey styling rather than overlaying an X.

note.summary: Permanent card upgrades stack as generated card mods.
note.details: Permanent card upgrades now live in progression.card_mods as base_card_id -> Array[mod], with legacy progression.card_upgrades kept only for compatibility. Decks, rewards, analytics ids, and saved runs still keep base card ids; callers resolve behavior through GameData.card_def_for_progression so generated stat/status/pattern/action mods can stack without adding collectible cards to the starting deck. The upgrade UI generates card -> element -> option choices from the current card definition, and costs scale by rarity plus per-card upgrade count.

note.summary: Door opening animation slices door_opening.png as 8 row-major frames from a padded 4x2 sheet.
note.details: The source sheet is 1774x887, so CombatBoardView intentionally derives 443x443 frame cells by integer-dividing 4 columns by 2 rows and ignores the tiny right/bottom padding. Side-edge doors use horizontally flipped frame textures, matching the static door_col orientation.

note.summary: Door opening animation normalizes each source frame onto a shared anchored canvas.
note.details: door_opening.png is not a reliable equal-cell sheet. CombatBoardView slices the alpha-tight sprite bounds, places each frame on the max 256x383 transparent canvas anchored bottom-right, and scales the shared canvas from frame 0's used height against the static door used rect. Source-oriented doors anchor right/bottom; flipped side doors anchor left/bottom so the door footprint does not resize or drift while opening.

note.summary: Labyrinth Crumble is generated from tools/build_labyrinth_font.py; regenerate font artifacts instead of hand-editing the TTF/TRES binaries.
note.details: The script emits fonts/LabyrinthCrumble-Regular.ttf, the embedded Godot FontFile .tres, and fonts/LabyrinthCrumble-preview.png from deterministic pixel glyph plans. The imagegen concept sheet is preserved as fonts/LabyrinthCrumble-reference.png for art direction.

note.summary: Labyrinth Crumble ships generated regular and header font faces; regenerate artifacts with tools/build_labyrinth_font.py.
note.details: The generator emits Regular and Header TTF/TRES files plus the preview PNG. Regular remains the default theme font, while UiTypography routes card names, button text, title/room headers, and enemy intent titles through the Header face for a heavier stone display style. Do not hand-edit generated font binaries.

note.summary: CombatBoardView board zoom now fits rendered tiles instead of the raw hidden-wall grid.
note.details: The board layout uses the extents of _tiles_in_draw_order() so hidden outer walls no longer make the room look smaller. Tile width can grow to 190px, and _board_origin biases the fitted content upward while preserving top clearance for unit HUDs and bottom clearance above the hand.

note.summary: CombatBoardView board zoom fits rendered tiles through a cached layout.
note.details: Board zoom still uses _tiles_in_draw_order() extents so hidden outer walls do not shrink the room, but those tiles/extents/tile width/origin must stay cached per state and Control.size. Calling _tiles_in_draw_order() or rebuilding extents from _tile_width()/_tile_center() hot draw paths caused severe animation and input lag.

note.summary: Generated PNG overhaul intentionally leaves character art references untouched.
note.details: Non-character card, icon, relic, tile loot, and UI chrome assets now live under assets/art as generated PNGs. Remaining SVG references under assets/placeholders/units are intentional because the user explicitly asked not to replace player, enemy, or NPC character art during this overhaul.

note.summary: CardWidget art sits on one continuous parchment frame with wrapped icon rows.
note.details: The current card frame uses assets/art/ui/card_frame.png as a texture stylebox while ArtFrame and DetailsPanel stay visually empty, so card art floats in the upper third and ability details share the parchment below. Keep dense icon-summary rows split into short segments; otherwise larger 26px ability icons can overflow narrow card interiors. The rarity indicator is now a visible bottom notch, so do not reserve vertical space by hiding rarity UI.

note.summary: Combat hand cards intentionally overlap, relying on CardWidget hover z-index.
note.details: RunScene sets HandBox separation negative only in combat and computes hand card width with that overlap included, so cards can be wider without spreading across the full bottom bar. CardWidget's local hover/selected lift sets z_index=20, which keeps the active card visually in front of its overlapping neighbors.

note.summary: Hand cards now fan through wrapper slots so hover lift stays local while the hand layout owns arch offsets and rotation.
Card art now uses a clipped keep-aspect-covered fill with larger default summary tokens that only step down when the rendered row count would exceed the details panel height.
Hand fan layout should reserve slightly less than the full arch height so edge cards can dip below the viewport, and slot z-order should stack left-to-right so the rightmost card stays uncovered.
Board-wide overlays should target board_content_global_rect rather than CombatBoardView.size.
note.details: CombatBoardView fills the entire StageRoot, while the rendered isometric tiles occupy a much smaller cached footprint. DeathEngulfOverlay uses board_content_global_rect() so full-board visual effects grow from the actual tile bounds instead of covering the whole stage immediately.

reason: DeathEngulfOverlay was changed to start from the full screen edge, so it no longer targets CombatBoardView.board_content_global_rect().

note.summary: Enemy placeholder SVGs for harrier and warden now resolve to generated transparent PNG unit art.
note.details: data/enemies.json points Bone Harrier and Ash Warden at assets/placeholders/units/harrier.png and warden.png. Heart Warden remains on heart_warden.svg by request because the boss is planned for a ground-up redesign; NPC/player SVG placeholders were not part of this enemy-only art pass.

note.summary: Harrier and warden enemy PNG art now include same-stem 8-frame idle sheets.
note.details: data/enemies.json points Bone Harrier and Ash Warden at assets/placeholders/units/harrier.png and warden.png. Their idle animation sheets live at harrier_idle.png and warden_idle.png as 4x2 row-major atlases with 1020x1020 frames; CombatBoardView auto-loads same-stem _idle sheets with the default layout. Heart Warden remains on heart_warden.svg by request because the boss is planned for a ground-up redesign.

note.summary: Harrier and warden idle sheets are generated pose animations, not static bob sheets.
note.details: assets/placeholders/units/harrier_idle.png and warden_idle.png are 4x2 row-major 1020px-frame atlases generated through imagegen, then component-extracted, chroma-keyed, hard-alpha cleaned, globally palette-normalized, and baseline-aligned. The sheets intentionally contain real weight-shift pose changes with feet anchored rather than simple translations of the static PNGs.

note.summary: Harrier and warden generated idle sheets were pruned into slower ping-pong loops to avoid jitter.
note.details: The raw imagegen 8-frame sheets looked spasm-y because the generated frames were unordered pose variants with inconsistent silhouette motion. The current harrier_idle.png and warden_idle.png keep only the most compatible generated poses, duplicate/reorder them into calmer ping-pong loops, and data/enemies.json sets both enemies to idle_frame_seconds 0.18. This is a salvage pass rather than a fully clean hand-authored animation; avoid restoring all raw generated frames unless regenerating from scratch.

note.summary: Harrier and warden keep static PNG art but intentionally have no idle sheets.
note.details: The generated imagegen idle animation experiment was removed because even the salvaged ping-pong loops were not good enough to keep. CombatBoardView auto-loads same-stem _idle sheets, so harrier_idle.png and warden_idle.png should remain absent until a better third-party-generated spritesheet is available; data/enemies.json now has no idle_frame_seconds overrides for those enemies.

note.summary: Harrier and warden now use third-party idle sheets through the same-stem loader.
note.details: assets/placeholders/units/harrier_idle.png and warden_idle.png are present again as 4x2 row-major 4080x2040 sheets with 1020px frames, so CombatBoardView auto-loads them from the harrier.png and warden.png art paths. The harrier sheet had 912 visible #ff00ff edge pixels; those were made transparent without otherwise altering the sheet.

note.summary: Use typed helpers for GDScript Array[T] literals to avoid stricter Windows type checks.
note.details: Godot on Windows rejected assigning a generic Array literal/conditional branch to Array[Vector2i] in blink shortcut previews. When a local, return value, or dictionary payload needs Array[Vector2i], build it with helpers such as _vector2i_array/_vector2i_values or an explicitly typed temporary before assignment.

note.summary: Elemental combat rooms now seed 2-3 central-biased traps.
note.details: Trap generation no longer scales max trap count down by depth. Combat and boss elemental rooms roll 2-3 traps, score candidates toward central traversal lanes, penalize edges/corner bands, avoid the player entry halo and occupied tiles, and keep modest spacing between traps.

note.summary: Campfire room actions now use a StageRoot context overlay instead of the bottom choice bar.
note.details: RunScene builds ContextChoiceOverlay dynamically under StageRoot and routes campfire Sit/Leave there via _add_context_choice_button; other modes still use BottomStack/ChoiceBar.

note.summary: Campfire bonfire art is rendered as presentation scene_props.
note.details: RunScene passes scene_props=[{kind='campfire_bonfire', tile=Vector2i(4,4)}] for campfire mode. CombatBoardView loads assets/art/tiles/campfire_bonfire.png and scales it from tile width so the transparent sprite sits over the central 3x3 floor tiles without becoming terrain.
